user_message: Before my call with Alpine Ski House, assess the risk. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-25 13:58 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Alpine Ski House renewal risk

Our team encountered some challenges with renewal risk. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 08:58 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for renewal risk. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-01-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on renewal risk from our last session.

[00:07:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:11:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:16:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:19:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:23:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:27:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:30:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:35:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:39:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
